for_sales.sales_text:
Bethany is a very rare flush decked Heard 28, so has a large uncluttered foredeck, standing headroom below, and true workboat lines. She has been in the same ownership for 14 years, having been maintained and upgraded to a very high standard, including the recent beta 30 inboard engine.

She has a full inventory of everything you'd need for cruising plus an excellent catspaw 8 nesting dinghy. She has a jib head topsail which is easily handled. The mainsail has 3 slab reefs. Both the staysail and jib are on Wykham-Martin furlers. With a slippery hull, new sails and a feathering propeller, she is very competitive when racing and a fast, dry cruising boat. She sleeps 5, a double in the fore peak, 2 full length settee berths and a pilot berth in the saloon yet is easily single handed with a tiller pilot and the excellent sea feather wind vane steering gear. The saloon seats 6 around the drop leaf table, the galley has a 2 burner spirit stove, a sink and cold water pump, and ample storage. The heads is uncommonly aft, handy underway and more private than amidships at night.

She's on the market due to lifestyle changes.

Bethany is a very rare flush deck version of a Heard 28, the absence of coach roof gives true workboat lines, full standing headroom bellow and lots of deck space. Set up for short handed cruising she’s easy to handle and remarkably quick,can sleep 5 adults with a functional galley and separate aft heads compartment. Bethany sports many improvements including a new beta 30 engine, new Noble mast, Darglow featherstream propellor, new set of sails, all over winter cover and much more. Everything you’d expect is on board.
